description: Your university rocketry team is traveling from your home launch site in Switzerland to the Spaceport America Cup in New Mexico. The team needs to know exactly how much higher the rocket will fly in the thinner, hotter air. Using the EPFL_BellaLui_2020.ork design file, compare two launch environments by creating and running two simulations. The first simulation, named 'Switzerland', should use an altitude of 400 m and a temperature of 15 °C. The second simulation, named 'New Mexico', should use an altitude of 1400 m and a temperature of 35 °C. For both, use default/auto-calculated pressure values. Export the standard flight data for both simulations to /home/ga/Documents/exports/switzerland.csv and /home/ga/Documents/exports/new_mexico.csv. Save the configured rocket design to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/environmental_analysis.ork. Finally, create a comparison report at /home/ga/Documents/exports/environmental_impact_report.txt documenting the simulated apogees for both locations and identifying which environment resulted in a higher apogee.
